steps.title: Use the sign restriction
steps.content: The square root $\sqrt{5a+6}$ cannot be negative. Since it equals $-12a$, the value of $-12a$ must be nonnegative, so $a\le0$.

steps.title: Test the negative value
steps.content: Use the matching negative choice. $$ f\bigl(-\frac{3}{16}\bigr)=\sqrt{5\bigl(-\frac{3}{16}\bigr)+6}=\sqrt{\frac{81}{16}}=\frac{9}{4} $$ Also, $$ -12\bigl(-\frac{3}{16}\bigr)=\frac{9}{4} $$ The answer is C.
